description_long: This is a rustic wooden cabin with vertical timber planks forming the walls, reinforced by horizontal and diagonal wooden beams. The roof is steeply pitched and covered with reddish, fish-scale patterned shingles, featuring decorative wooden crossbeams at each gable end. The cabin sits on a raised stone foundation, giving it a sturdy and elevated appearance. There is a single rectangular window and a door on the main facade, with a set of wooden steps and a short walkway leading up to the entrance. The side and rear facades are plain, with no windows or doors visible, emphasizing the utilitarian design. The overall structure suggests a traditional, hand-built style suitable for rural or early urban environments.
description: A rustic wooden cabin with a steep red shingle roof, stone foundation, and a single front door and window. Decorative beams and a raised entrance add to its traditional, sturdy appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main facade with a single window, a door, and the stone foundation.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the plain wooden wall, decorative roof beams, and the absence of windows or doors.
description_view_2: Front view with visible wooden steps and walkway leading to the door, highlighting the entrance.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing a plain wooden wall with no openings and decorative roof beams.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with steep pitch, decorative crossbeams, and red fish-scale shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: one medium-sized rectangular window on the front facade, asymmetrically placed to the left of the door; no other windows visible
attribute_door_placement: one exterior door on the front facade, offset to the right, accessed by wooden steps and a walkway
